summary: All spec requirements for scheduler-backends-gcp are fully implemented and verified. R4: scheduler/mod.rs correctly declares #[cfg(feature = "cloud-scheduler")] pub mod cloud_scheduler_backend and re-exports CloudSchedulerBackend + CloudSchedulerConfig, following the same pattern as the existing ion_backend. R5: CloudSchedulerBackend implements all 5 SchedulerBackend trait methods (acquire_leader, renew_leader, release_leader, get_task_state, set_task_state); backend.rs is untouched. R6: cloud-scheduler feature is independent of scheduler (Ion); reqwest is optional; all three backends coexist. The spec Test Plan section exists but is a placeholder (<!-- TODO -->, 0 test cases defined); the Hard Reject Rule's second condition is not met because the diff contains 15 test functions in backends_gcp_tests covering S1/S3/S4/R5/R6, plus 45+ tests in cloud_scheduler_backend_tests.rs. cargo test -p cclab-queue --features cloud-scheduler: 196 passed, 0 failed.

code_matches_spec.notes: R4 (module registration): scheduler/mod.rs:13-14 — #[cfg(feature = "cloud-scheduler")] pub mod cloud_scheduler_backend. scheduler/mod.rs:22-23 — #[cfg(feature = "cloud-scheduler")] pub use cloud_scheduler_backend::{CloudSchedulerBackend, CloudSchedulerConfig}. Pattern mirrors ion_backend at lines 10-11 and 20-21. R5 (SchedulerBackend trait compatibility): cloud_scheduler_backend.rs:512-567 — impl SchedulerBackend for CloudSchedulerBackend with all 5 required methods: acquire_leader (no-op, returns true), renew_leader (no-op, returns true), release_leader (no-op, returns Ok(())), get_task_state (RwLock HashMap read), set_task_state (RwLock HashMap write). backend.rs not in diff — SchedulerBackend trait and TaskScheduleState struct are unchanged. R6 (feature isolation): cclab-queue/Cargo.toml:99 — cloud-scheduler = ["dep:reqwest"] is independent of scheduler (Ion) and cloudtasks features. reqwest is optional. gcp-full = ["gcp-push", "cloud-scheduler"] at line 110. Spec note about base64 is satisfied: base64 is a non-optional workspace dep (Cargo.toml:58), so no feature activation needed. Additional bonus: lib.rs:93-94 also re-exports CloudSchedulerBackend + CloudSchedulerConfig at crate root, which goes beyond the spec requirement but is architecturally consistent.

tests_present.notes: Spec has a ## Test Plan section (scheduler-backends-gcp.md:103-106) but it is an empty placeholder (<!-- TODO -->, 0 test cases defined). The Hard Reject Rule's second condition is false: the diff contains well-populated test functions. scheduler/mod.rs defines #[cfg(all(test, feature = "cloud-scheduler"))] mod backends_gcp_tests with 15 test functions: s1_cloud_scheduler_backend_importable, s1_cloud_scheduler_config_importable, s1_cloud_scheduler_module_reexports, s3_cloud_scheduler_satisfies_scheduler_backend_trait, s3_trait_object_acquire_leader, s3_trait_object_renew_leader, s3_trait_object_release_leader, s3_trait_object_get_task_state, s3_trait_object_set_task_state, s3_trait_object_all_methods_in_sequence, s4_memory_and_cloud_backends_coexist, s4_backends_interchangeable_at_runtime, s4_backends_independent_state, r5_task_schedule_state_unchanged, r6_memory_backend_unaffected_by_cloud_feature. All 15 passed.

no_regressions.notes: cargo test -p cclab-queue --features cloud-scheduler: 196 passed, 0 failed, 14 ignored. All pre-existing tests (NATS, Redis, Pub/Sub, workflow, scheduler) continue to pass. No compilation errors or warnings related to this change.

findings.area: spec quality — Test Plan section is an empty placeholder
findings.issue: scheduler-backends-gcp.md ## Test Plan section contains only <!-- TODO -->
findings.detail: scheduler-backends-gcp.md:103-106 — the Test Plan section header exists with a type annotation but no test cases are defined. The 15 unit tests in backends_gcp_tests cover all 4 scenarios (S1/S3/S4) and requirements (R4/R5/R6) with explicit labels, but the mapping is not formalized in the spec.
findings.required_fix: Not blocking for implementation approval. Recommend filling in the Test Plan section in a follow-up spec fill cycle to formalize the scenario-to-test-function mapping.

positive_observations: R4 module registration follows the existing ion_backend pattern exactly — consistent style makes it easy to identify all conditional backends at a glance
R5 leader election no-ops (acquire/renew always return true, release is Ok(())) correctly model GCP Cloud Scheduler's role as the single scheduling authority — no self-hosted locking needed
R5 in-memory task state tracking via tokio::sync::RwLock<HashMap<String, TaskScheduleState>> is safe, correct, and consistent with the MemorySchedulerBackend approach
R6 feature isolation is airtight: reqwest is optional and only activated by cloud-scheduler; the feature does not depend on scheduler (Ion) at all

bonus pause_task/resume_task overrides in the SchedulerBackend impl correctly sync both GCP API (pause_job_api/resume_job_api) and local state — spec did not require this but it is architecturally sound
Test functions are explicitly labeled with scenario IDs (s1_, s3_, s4_, r5_, r6_) matching spec requirements — traceability is excellent
15 targeted tests all pass in 0.03s — fast, hermetic, no network dependency
196 total tests pass with 0 failures confirming no regressions across the full crate
